title: Sum of Array
description:
Given an array of N integers, find the sum of all elements.

Input:
The first line contains a single integer N — the number of elements.
The second line contains N space-separated integers a1, a2, ..., aN.

Output:
Print a single integer — the sum of all elements in the array.

boilerplateCode.python:
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
# Compute and print the sum

title: Reverse Array
description:
Given an array of N integers, print the array in reverse order.

Input:
The first line contains a single integer N — the number of elements.
The second line contains N space-separated integers.

Output:
Print N space-separated integers — the array in reverse order.

boilerplateCode.python:
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
# Reverse and print space-separated

title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of N integers and a target value T, find two distinct indices i and j such that arr[i] + arr[j] = T. Print the two indices (0-indexed) in ascending order.

It is guaranteed that exactly one solution exists.

Input:
The first line contains two integers N and T — the number of elements and the target sum.
The second line contains N space-separated integers.

Output:
Print two space-separated integers — the indices i and j (0-indexed, i < j) such that arr[i] + arr[j] = T.

boilerplateCode.python:
n, target = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
# Find two indices that sum to target
# Print: i j (space-separated, 0-indexed, i < j)

title: Maximum Subarray Sum
description:
Given an array of N integers, find the maximum sum of any contiguous subarray.

Input:
The first line contains a single integer N — the number of elements.
The second line contains N space-separated integers.

Output:
Print a single integer — the maximum subarray sum.

boilerplateCode.python:
n = int(input())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
# Find maximum subarray sum (Kadane's Algorithm)

title: Binary Search
description:
Given a sorted array of N distinct integers and Q queries, for each query find the 0-based index of the target element. If the element is not found, print -1.

Input:
The first line contains two integers N and Q — the size of the array and the number of queries.
The second line contains N space-separated integers in sorted (ascending) order.
Each of the next Q lines contains a single integer — the target to search for.

Output:
For each query, print the 0-based index of the target element, or -1 if not found. Each answer on a new line.

boilerplateCode.python:
n, q = map(int, input().split())
arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
# For each query, binary search and print index or -1
for _ in range(q):
